page: Amazon Web Services c4.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance belonging to the c4 family, powered by the Xen hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.9 GHz. The configuration includes 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The processor is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and 25 MB of L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA.

The instance is provisioned with 30.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.88 GB. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be handled via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier across multiple workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1182.0 and a multi-core score of 7354.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 15674.33. In memory-bound operations, it registers a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 851.49 GB/sec and an uncached large block write speed of 47.18 GB/sec. For database and application workloads, the instance processes 6.16 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers 11.52 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. Large language model inference speeds are moderate, processing 872.74 tokens per second for a small 135M model prompt, but dropping to a weak 23.37 tokens per second for a medium 7B model prompt. This hardware profile is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and medium-scale batch processing where high memory density is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services c4.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance designed for CPU-intensive workloads. Powered by the Xen hypervisor, it features a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 2.9 GHz clock speed. The hardware configuration includes 30.0 GB of memory, a 25 MB L3 cache, and support for AVX and FMA instruction sets. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 15674.33 and a Redis SET throughput of 6.16 million operations per second. While it handles small-model LLM inference efficiently, performance drops on larger models. This instance is best suited for compute-heavy applications such as software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c4.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 30.0 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor, designed for CPU-heavy workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c4.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 30.0 GB RAM, and a 2.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized virtual server featuring sixteen vCPUs and thirty gigabytes of memory for CPU-intensive processing tasks.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores
30.0 GB of system memory optimized for compute-heavy workloads
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or bundled GPUs
Delivers 6.16 million Redis SET operations per second and 11.52 GB/sec web serving throughput
Suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving
